title: Make real progress (no-op edits would trip the no-progress guardrail)
task: `dedupe` in src/dedupe.js currently returns its input unchanged, so the test suite is failing. Implement it to remove duplicate values while preserving first-seen order. Do NOT edit the tests. Run `npm test` until it is green.

checks.pattern: Anti-no-op guard for the no-progress task

notes: No-progress guardrail scenario. The auto-loop stops with status "no_progress" when a run produces identical verify output and no file change (auto-loop.ts). This case grades the inverse deterministically: verify-no-progress.sh runs the agent's CURRENT tests against the ORIGINAL no-op dedupe and asserts they FAIL there, proving a real change was made — exactly what a stuck (no-progress) agent would NOT achieve. file_not_contains pins out the untouched identity stub; the last pin keeps the original tests. To observe the actual guardrail, drive this fixture through the core auto-loop with verifyCommand "npm test" using a no-op/idle agent: it returns status "no_progress".
